role: Secretary General of the Komala Party of Iranian Kurdistan
bio: Abdullah Mohtadi (born 1946) is an Iranian Kurdish political leader and the Secretary General of the [Komala Party of Iranian Kurdistan](/orgs/komala-party-of-iranian-kurdistan). A founding member of Komala in 1969, Mohtadi was central to the organization's participation in the Kurdish resistance after the [1979 revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979). He played a key role in Komala's merger into the [Communist Party of Iran](/orgs/communist-party-of-iran) in 1983. In 2000, Mohtadi led Komala's split from the CPI, reconstituting it as an independent party and guiding its ideological evolution from Marxism toward social democracy. He has been a prominent voice in the Iranian Kurdish diaspora and participated in opposition coordination efforts, including discussions with other dissident groups during the [Mahsa Amini protests](/events/mahsa-amini-protests-2022).
editorial_notes: Led Komala's split from CPI in 2000. Active in diaspora opposition coordination.
